name: Salvador (Brazil)

colors.symbolism: the field of the flag, derived from the city's heraldic banner of arms
the dove and olive branch of the Holy Spirit
the olive branch held by the dove

description: A blue field charged in the centre with a white dove holding a green olive branch in its beak — a banner of arms taken from the central charge of the city's coat of arms.
history: Salvador ("São Salvador da Bahía de Todos os Santos"), founded in 1549 as Brazil's first colonial capital, takes its banner of arms from the central charge of its historic coat of arms: a dove of the Holy Spirit (Espírito Santo) carrying an olive branch — a reference to the city's full Catholic name and to the Pentecost.

features.symbolism: the blue field of the city's banner of arms
the white dove of the Holy Spirit (Espírito Santo) recalling Salvador's full original name São Salvador da Bahía de Todos os Santos and the city's strong Catholic identity

features.role: olive branch held in the dove's beak
features.symbolism: the olive branch held by the dove, an emblem of peace and of the Holy Spirit's descent at Pentecost
